lesson_meta.title: AFFECTION (A TRUE FRIEND, ADVERTISEMENT & BIOGRAPHY WRITING)

sections.title: Summary
sections.content: This section of Unit 1 ('Affection') features a story titled 'A True Friend', highlighting the beautiful bond between two childhood friends, Nandhini and Pandi. It explores themes of friendship, empathy, and making others happy. When Nandhini is unusually sad and silent because she lost two front teeth, Pandi tries his best to make her smile, eventually succeeding with a rubber frog. The text also includes a reading comprehension activity based on a TV advertisement and a writing exercise to draft a biographical paragraph about the prominent leader Kamarajar.

sections.title: Section 1: A True Friend (Supplementary Story)
content: Nandhini and Pandi were good friends since childhood and lived on the same street.
They walked to school together, chatting, playing, and sometimes racing home.
They participated in school activities together and helped each other with lessons.
One day, Nandhini looked very upset and sad. When Pandi asked why or if her parents scolded her, she only shook her head.
Pandi tried to cheer her up by grabbing her bag and saying, 'Catch me if you can', but she did not respond.
In class, Nandhini sat with her head bowed down, her eyes glued to the ground, and didn't answer her friends.
When the teacher called for attendance, Nandhini did not answer, making the teacher annoyed.
The teacher called her name louder. Nandhini raised her hand but still didn't answer.
The teacher touched her forehead to check for a fever because her cheeks were red. Nandhini just shook her head to indicate she didn't have a fever.
Her friends pestered her with a hundred questions about her family, house, shoes, and pets. She answered every question only by shaking her head.
Pandi desperately wanted to make her smile. He thought for hours and finally came up with an idea.
He took something from his bag, ran towards her, tumbled, and the object flew towards Nandhini.
Nandhini caught it with a 'THUD!'. It was a big green frog.
Everyone else ran away scared, but Nandhini's eyes opened wide with surprise, and she burst into laughter.
It was a rubber frog!
When she laughed, everyone finally knew the reason for her sadness and silence: Two of her front teeth were missing!

sections.title: Section 2: Let Us Read Aloud (Advertisement Comprehension)
content: The section includes an advertisement for 'Bright Vision Televisions'.
The offer is named 'WATCH & CLICK OFFER'.
If you buy an LED TV, you get a CAMERA FREE.

sections.title: Section 3: Let Us Write - Biographical Sketch
content: Information provided to write a narrative paragraph on Kamarajar:
Name: Kamarajar
Known as: King maker, Karma veerar
Birth Date: 15th July 1903
Place of birth: Virudhunagar, Tamilnadu
Position held: Chief minister of Tamil Nadu
Achievements: Midday Meal Scheme, Reforms in Education
Honour/Award: 'Bharat Ratna' (1976)
Death Date: 2nd October 1975

sections.title: Book Back Exercises (One Mark Questions)
content: A. Name the character or speaker:
1. 'Do you have a fever?' -> The teacher
2. 'Did someone scold you?' -> One of her friends (also asked by Pandi earlier)
3. 'Why does Nandhini look so sad?' -> Her friends
4. 'Are you alright?' -> (Implied concern from the teacher who asked 'Are you feeling okay?')
B. Number the actions of Pandi and Nandhini in order:
1. Nandhini and Pandi were friends. (1)
2. Nandhini looked terribly upset one day. (2)
3. She did not respond to Pandi's play. (3)
4. Friends pestered her why she was upset. (4)
5. She did not respond to the attendance. (5)
6. Pandi threw a rubber frog on her. (6)
7. She burst into laughter and all know the reason. (7)
C. Let Us Read Aloud (Advertisement Questions):
1. What is the advertisement about? -> Bright Vision Televisions (LED TV).
2. What is free, for LED TV? -> A Camera.
(Note: Questions 3, 4, 5 depend on visual details in the ad image that are not fully transcribed in the text snippet, but the main points are covered above).

questions.question: Who are the two main friends in the story?
questions.explanation: The story begins by introducing Nandhini and Pandi as good friends since childhood.

questions.question: How long have Nandhini and Pandi been friends?
questions.explanation: The text states they were good friends since childhood.

questions.question: Where did Nandhini and Pandi live?
questions.explanation: The text mentions, 'They lived in the same street, so they were always together.'

questions.question: What did Pandi do every day on the way to school?
questions.explanation: Every day Pandi would stop at her home on the way to school.

questions.question: How did they usually travel to school?
questions.explanation: The text says, 'Both would walk to school chatting and playing.'

questions.question: What did Pandi ask Nandhini when she looked upset initially?
questions.explanation: When he noticed she was upset, he directly asked, 'Why are you sad?'

questions.question: How did Nandhini respond to Pandi's initial questions?
questions.explanation: She did not answer him verbally; instead, she shook her head.

questions.question: What playful thing did Pandi do to try and get Nandhini to respond on the way to school?
options: He grabbed her bag and said 'Catch me if you can'
questions.explanation: He grabbed her bag excitedly hoping she would chase him and play.

questions.question: How did Nandhini walk into the classroom?
options: Slowly, with eyes glued to the ground
questions.explanation: The text describes her walking slowly into the classroom with her eyes glued to the ground.

questions.question: Who asked Nandhini, 'Did someone scold you?' in the classroom?
questions.explanation: In the classroom, one of her friends asked this question.

questions.question: Why did the teacher get annoyed during attendance?
options: Because Nandhini did not answer when her name was called
questions.explanation: The teacher was annoyed when Nandhini failed to answer the attendance call.

questions.question: What did Nandhini do when the teacher called her name louder?
options: She raised her hand but did not answer
She ran out of the class
questions.explanation: She acknowledged her presence by raising her hand, but still remained silent.

questions.question: Why did the teacher think Nandhini might have a fever?
questions.explanation: The teacher touched her forehead because her cheeks were red, making it look like she had a fever.

questions.question: What did the teacher offer to do when she thought Nandhini was sick?
questions.explanation: The teacher asked, 'Do you have a fever? Shall I call your parents?'

questions.question: What kind of questions did Nandhini's friends pester her with?
options: Questions about her family, house, shoes, and street pets
Questions about what she ate for lunch
questions.explanation: Her friends pestered her with a hundred questions about everything, including her family, house, shoes, and pets.

questions.question: Why did Pandi want an idea?
questions.explanation: Pandi had never seen her so dull and wanted an idea to make her smile.

questions.question: What flew straight towards Nandhini from Pandi's hands?
questions.explanation: When Pandi tumbled, a rubber frog slipped from his hands and flew towards her.

questions.question: What was Nandhini's initial reaction when she saw the object flying towards her?
questions.explanation: She saw something flying towards her and screamed with fear.

questions.question: Why did everyone else in the class run away?
options: They were scared of the big green frog
questions.explanation: Everyone ran away as they were scared of the frog, thinking it was real.

questions.question: What was the actual reason Nandhini was sad and silent all day?
options: Two of her front teeth were missing
She had a fight with Pandi
questions.explanation: When she finally laughed at the rubber frog, everyone saw that two of her front teeth were missing, which is why she didn't want to open her mouth.

questions.question: In the advertisement provided, what is the name of the dealer selling televisions?
questions.explanation: The text at the top of the advertisement clearly states 'Bright Vision Televisions'.

questions.question: According to the advertisement, what free gift do you get when you buy an LED TV?
questions.explanation: The advertisement boldly says 'GET A CAMERA FREE'.

questions.question: What scheme is Kamarajar famous for introducing in schools?
questions.explanation: Under the 'Achievements' section for writing his biography, 'Midday Meal Scheme' is listed.

questions.question: When was Kamarajar born?
questions.explanation: The 'Birth' detail provided is 15th July 1903.

questions.question: What is the birthplace of Kamarajar?
questions.explanation: The details state his place of birth is Virudhunagar, Tamilnadu.

questions.question: By what titles was Kamarajar known?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'Known as' as 'King maker' and 'Karma veerar'.

questions.question: What prestigious honour was awarded to Kamarajar in 1976?
questions.explanation: The details mention 'Honour' as 'Bharat Ratna' (1976).

questions.question: Which position did Kamarajar hold according to the text?
questions.explanation: The 'Position held' is listed as Chief minister of Tamil Nadu.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): The teacher touched Nandhini's forehead.
Reason (R): Nandhini had red cheeks and the teacher suspected she might have a fever.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The teacher observed her red cheeks and touched her forehead to check for a fever.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Pandi intentionally threw a real frog at Nandhini to scare her.
Reason (R): Pandi was angry that she was ignoring him all day.
options: Both A and R are true
Both A and R are false
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The frog was a rubber frog, not real. Pandi threw it (or it slipped) to make her smile, not out of anger.

questions.question: Who is the speaker of these words: 'Catch me if you can.'?
questions.explanation: Pandi said this when he grabbed her bag to try and playfully cheer her up.

questions.question: What sound did Nandhini make when she caught the frog?
questions.explanation: The text describes her catching it with a 'THUD!'.

questions.question:
Complete the analogy based on Kamarajar's biographical details.
Birth : 15th July 1903 :: Death : ________
questions.explanation: The detail for his death is given as 2nd October 1975.

questions.question: Find the odd one out among Kamarajar's details.
questions.explanation: King maker and Karma veerar are titles. Bharat Ratna is an award. Virudhunagar is a place name.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'eyes glued to the ground' mean in the context of Nandhini walking?
options: She was looking for a dropped coin
She had literal glue on her face
She was looking down continuously, feeling shy or sad
She was trying to avoid stepping on bugs
questions.explanation: It's an idiom meaning she kept her gaze fixed downwards because she was upset and hiding.

questions.question: How did Nandhini react to all the pestering questions from her friends?
options: She answered only by shaking her head
questions.explanation: She didn't want to open her mouth, so she answered each question just by shaking her head.

questions.question:
Match the event to the order it happened in the story:
A) Pandi threw a rubber frog on her.
B) She did not respond to the attendance.
C) Nandhini looked terribly upset one day.
questions.explanation: First she looked upset (C), later in class she didn't respond to attendance (B), and finally Pandi threw the frog (A).

questions.question: According to the story, what did Nandhini and Pandi sometimes do on their way home?
questions.explanation: The text states, 'Sometimes, they would race home from school.'

questions.question: HOTS: Why do you think Nandhini chose to remain silent the whole day just because her teeth were missing?
options: She wanted to trick the teacher.
She felt embarrassed or self-conscious about her appearance without her front teeth.
questions.explanation: Children often feel embarrassed when they lose their front teeth because it changes their smile visibly.

questions.question: HOTS: What does Pandi's effort to make Nandhini smile tell us about his character?
options: He is very annoying and likes to trouble people.
He doesn't care about the teacher's rules.
He is an observant and caring friend who goes out of his way to help a friend in distress.
questions.explanation: He thought for hours just to find a way to break her sadness, demonstrating deep empathy and true friendship.

questions.question: HOTS: The title is 'A True Friend'. Who is the primary 'true friend' being referred to in the context of solving the problem in the story?
options: The friend who asked 'Did someone scold you?'
questions.explanation: While they are both friends, the story highlights Pandi's persistent effort to cheer Nandhini up, embodying the qualities of a true friend.

questions.question: If you are writing a biography of Kamarajar based on the given points, under which heading would you place 'Reforms in Education'?
questions.explanation: The text categorizes 'Reforms in Education' alongside the Midday Meal Scheme under 'Achievements'.

questions.question: What caused the students to realize the real reason for Nandhini's silence?
options: The teacher forced her to speak
She wrote it on the blackboard
She burst into laughter, opening her mouth
questions.explanation: When the surprise of the rubber frog made her laugh out loud, everyone could finally see her missing teeth.

questions.question: In the sequence of actions, what happens immediately after Nandhini does not respond to Pandi's playful attempt with the bag?
options: They walk silently to the school.
questions.explanation: After she doesn't respond to 'Catch me if you can', Pandi gives the bag back and they walk silently to school.

questions.question: Why did the teacher call Nandhini's name louder during attendance?
options: Because Nandhini didn't answer the first time
Because it was a special announcement
Because she was sitting at the back
questions.explanation: The teacher was annoyed when Nandhini didn't answer the first time, so she called her name louder the second time.

questions.question: HOTS: If Pandi hadn't used the rubber frog, what might have happened for the rest of the day?
options: Nandhini would have likely remained silent and sad, and her friends would have kept worrying.
The teacher would have expelled her.
questions.explanation: Since she was determined not to show her teeth, only a sudden, genuine surprise could break her conscious effort to stay silent.

questions.question: Which action shows that Nandhini was trying to catch the object flying towards her despite being scared?
options: She closed her eyes and tried to catch it
She hit it with a book
questions.explanation: The text states: 'She closed her eyes and tried to catch it. THUD! She caught it.'

questions.question: What is the primary message of the story 'A True Friend'?
options: A true friend notices your sadness and tries hard to make you happy again.
questions.explanation: The story emphasizes Pandi's concern and his successful effort to cheer up his distressed friend.

questions.question: HOTS: Compare the reactions of the other students and Nandhini to the flying frog. What is the difference?
options: Everyone ran away scared, but Nandhini caught it and eventually laughed.
Everyone caught it, but Nandhini ran away.
There was no difference; everyone laughed immediately.
questions.explanation: The others assumed it was real and fled, whereas Nandhini, who was the target, reflexively caught it and then realized the joke.

questions.question: Based on the TV advertisement details provided, what is the 'Watch & Click' offer implying?
options: You watch the TV and click the remote.
You watch TV (by buying one) and click photos (with the free camera).
You watch an ad and click a link online.
You click your fingers to turn on the TV.
questions.explanation: The offer combines watching (Televisions) with clicking (getting a Camera free).
